behavior_summary: Provides the 'U8P_VIVALTER::look' (examine) handler for an altar object: when examined it composes the literal label "altar ", asks the item/process to 'bark' that label so the player sees the altar name/description, then signals the U8P_VIVALTER::look succeeded.
The handler does not modify global game flags or inventories; its sole gameplay effect is producing the UI text for examination and returning a success implication for the U8P_VIVALTER::look action.
notable_control_flow: Push the object/process reference (inferred from the event parameter) and the string, then call Item::bark to display the label.
Free the constructed string buffer after the call.
Use the bark call return value and an 'implies' operation to indicate the U8P_VIVALTER::look succeeded, then suspend/return the handler.
open_questions: Is the pushed [BP+06h] parameter the altar object instance (the thing being examined) or the viewer's process id? (analysis infers it's the object/process reference to which bark is directed).
Does Item::bark return any status beyond success/failure that affects the implies semantics in downstream logic?
Are there any locale/grammar variants of the label beyond the static literal "altar " that could be selected by other code paths?

main_function.name: U8P_VIVALTER::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_VIVALTER::look' event for the altar object by producing and displaying the altar label and indicating the U8P_VIVALTER::look succeeded.
entry_conditions: Event 0 / 'U8P_VIVALTER::look' — invoked when a player or actor examines/looks at the altar object (the object's U8P_VIVALTER::look handler is triggered).

function_map.name: U8P_VIVALTER::look
function_map.role: Look handler for an altar item that composes and emits a short label via Item::bark.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to display short text/messages associated with an item/process.

external_calls.purpose: Request the item/process to display a short text label (here the literal "altar ") to the player or viewing process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the 'U8P_VIVALTER::look' (examine) handler for an altar object: when examined it composes the literal label "altar ", asks the item/process to 'bark' that label so the player sees the altar name/description, then signals the U8P_VIVALTER::look succeeded.
